policy_statement: TMG will elevate Shinozaki Park to secure evacuation routes to the Edogawa Levee.
mechanism_description: Elevating the park helps secure evacuation routes to the levee, improving flood response and evacuation resilience.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard (flooding) → ↓ exposure/↑ response capacity (evacuation access maintained) → ↓ climate impact risk
causal_mechanism_detail: (1) Change: Elevate park ground/grades and associated access ways to keep routes to levees/evacuation points usable during high water. (2) Process: Higher elevations remain above floodwaters longer, reducing inundation depth/duration on critical paths and preventing route cut-offs. (3) Outcome: Faster/safer evacuation and improved emergency access, lowering flood-related casualties and damages.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced loss of life and damages during floods by maintaining passable evacuation routes and access to flood defenses.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: flood hazard → maintained evacuation routes/response capacity → reduced impacts (risk reduction).  
2) Why Adaptation over alternatives: The mechanism targets coping capacity and exposure during floods, not emissions reduction (Mitigation), not primarily reducing energy/material inputs (Resource Efficiency), and not relying on ecosystem processes as the main lever (Nature-Based Solutions).  
3) Edge cases applied: If elevation were achieved via wetland restoration or other ecological flood buffering, it could implicate Nature-Based Solutions; here the described lever is physical elevation to secure routes, so Adaptation remains primary.  
4) Applies to ALL instances sharing this mechanism: Any policy that elevates parks/grounds to secure evacuation routes functions mainly as flood-response resilience and exposure reduction.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment (e.g., “critical infrastructure resilience,” “evacuation planning,” “flood risk management”) is deferred to policy-level evidence and implementation specifics.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If the park elevation is explicitly implemented as a “green” flood storage/raised landscape that also provides significant flood attenuation or carbon sequestration via ecosystem restoration, a secondary Nature-Based Solutions tag might be warranted at the policy level; primary remains Adaptation for the stated mechanism.
key_indicators: "elevate Shinozaki Park"; "secure evacuation routes"; "to the Edogawa Levee"
co_benefits: Improved park usability/amenity and general emergency preparedness (non-climate disasters)
instance_edge_case_notes: No explicit mention of flooding in the verbatim text, but the evacuation-route-to-levee context strongly implies flood adaptation; classified as direct based on the infrastructure elevation measure.
